summary: Surah Al-Haqqah portrays the Inevitable: past nations like Thamud, Aad, and Pharaoh were destroyed for denial. It describes the blast of the Trumpet, the upheaval of earth and sky, and the Throne borne by eight. People receive their records in the right hand or left, determining bliss or torment. The surah asserts the Qur’an is revelation—not poetry or soothsaying—and warns that fabrication is impossible, ending with glorifying the Lord Most Great.

groups.title: Section: Past deniers destroyed: Thamud by a Cry, Aad by a furious wind; Pharaoh and the overturned dwellings seized
